description:
LRR Phase 10 §3.1 — per-condition LLM call slicing. Every LLM call site (director loop + 11 per-agent sites from #961, #966) publishes hapax_llm_calls_total / hapax_llm_call_latency_seconds / hapax_llm_call_outcomes_total tagged with the active research condition_id. This dashboard makes the behavior distinction between conditions visible for Bayesian analysis (PyMC MCMC BEST per the Cycle 2 pre-registration at osf.io/5c2kr).

Conditions of interest as of 2026-04-17:
- cond-phase-a-baseline-qwen-001 (pre-persona-doc apparatus, closed)
- cond-phase-a-persona-doc-qwen-001 (current, post-Phase-7 persona-document-driven voice + director)

Routes correspond to call-site purposes, not LiteLLM tier names:
  director, conversation, spontaneous-speech, workspace-analyzer,
  screen-analyzer, eval-judge, vision-tool, scan-extract, vision-observer,
  dmn-sensory, dmn-thinking, dmn-multimodal.

Deployment: symlink or copy this file to ~/llm-stack/grafana/provisioning/dashboards/json/lrr-per-condition.json, then restart the grafana container or reload provisioning via the Grafana UI. No live-reload watch — file changes require a provisioning reload.

panels.description: Call rate per route, stacked. Volume regression check — did any call site drop to zero?
panels.title: Call rate by route (req/s)

panels.description: p95 latency by condition. Per-condition substrate-performance comparison — the bar chart on the right in the Cycle 2 pre-reg.
panels.title: Latency p50 / p95 by condition

panels.description: Outcome distribution by route. Refusal or error rate spikes on any single route indicate substrate / gate regression. The set-outcome('refused') calls from HTTP-non-200 responses in dmn/ollama.py land here.
panels.title: Outcome mix by route (%)

panels.description: Per-condition call volume table. Fast-glance summary of which condition has accumulated how much traffic — when Phase A baseline sample is large enough for Bayesian analysis, the number-of-calls column signals readiness.
panels.title: Total calls by condition × route

panels.description: Refusal rate (refusals / total) over time, per route. A threshold spike on any one route (director, dmn-*, vision-*) indicates substrate or routing regression. Compare against condition transitions in the left panel.
panels.title: Refusal rate by route

panels.description: Error + timeout rate (non-success, non-refused) by condition. Substrate instability signal — a per-condition error rate trend separates apparatus changes (condition branching) from infrastructure problems (which would show up across conditions).
panels.title: Error + timeout rate by condition

title: LRR — Per-condition LLM call slicing
